personal.name: Your Name
personal.title: Full Stack Systems Engineer
personal.tagline: I build performant systems and playful interfaces.

about: Welcome to my city — literally. I'm a software engineer studying at The Ohio State University.
Every street, tower and tunnel here is generated from a deterministic Perlin-noise seed, and every data node you break in the undercity is a real skill from my resume.
Head right to explore my career: Market Street holds my frontend work, the Industrial Zone my backend systems, and the Mega-Corp Tower my flagship project.

skills.description: Strict typing across full-stack apps, generics, and compiler-driven refactoring.
Hooks, concurrent features, performance profiling, and design-system components.
Data pipelines, scripting, FastAPI services, and algorithm implementation.
WebGL 2D rendering, arcade physics, chunked tilemaps — this very city runs on it.
Lambda, S3, CloudFront, IAM — serverless architectures and CDN deployment.
Express/Fastify APIs, streaming, worker threads, and build tooling.
Postgres schema design, query optimization, and migrations.
Containerized builds, GitHub Actions pipelines, and automated deployment.
Object-oriented design, data structures, and coursework foundations.

dialogue: State your business. Ah — a recruiter. I directed this engineer's work on our flagship platform.
They shipped features used by thousands of users and never missed a sprint commitment.
The tower behind me is a monument to that work. Step through the lobby gate to review the file.
Hey, welcome to the old block! I mentored this developer during their first internship.
They arrived knowing syntax and left understanding systems — the difference matters.
Break into the undercity below to see the skills they mined along the way.
Best interfaces on the strip, friend! I worked with this developer on client-facing builds.
Pixel-perfect components, accessible flows, and animations that never dropped a frame.
Everything glowing on this street is a UI they could have shipped.
This foundry still runs the old systems... COBOL-era schemas, monoliths, legacy APIs.
This developer learned to respect what came before while building what comes next.
The machine hall holds the migration records. Mind the conveyor.

signs.text: ◢ RESIDENTIAL SECTOR 07 — Walk right with A/D or arrows. SPACE to jump, SHIFT to dash, CLICK to break blocks, E to interact.
SCRAPYARD — decommissioned: jQuery spaghetti (2019), PHP 5 sites (2020), FTP deployments (2021). Recycled, learned from.
◈ MARKET STREET — frontend & UI/UX district. Talk to the vendors; every neon sign is a shipped interface.
⚠ INDUSTRIAL ZONE — backend architecture & legacy systems run in the foundry. Authorized personnel only (you're authorized).
▲ MEGA-CORP TOWER — flagship projects archive. Press E at the lobby gate to view the portfolio.
⛏ THE UNDERCITY — break glowing data nodes to collect skills into your inventory (I).
☢ EXCLUSION ZONE — end of the grid. Future goals & open source lie in the ruins ahead...

projects.name: Flagship Enterprise Platform
projects.summary: A production web platform serving real users, with CI/CD, monitoring, and a component design system.
highlights: Cut deployment time by 40% by rebuilding the CI pipeline in GitHub Actions
Designed a typed event bus decoupling three frontend teams' code
Led performance work that took p95 page load from 4.2s to 1.1s

projects.name: This Portfolio (Meta!)
projects.summary: The city you are standing in: deterministic Perlin-noise terrain, chunk streaming, Light2D neon, and a React/Phaser event-bus architecture.
highlights: Chunked tilemap streaming keeps memory flat on an effectively large world
Perlin noise with constant seed — same city every visit, zero randomness
Hybrid rendering: WebGL game canvas under an accessible React DOM overlay

projects.name: Legacy Systems Migration
projects.summary: Migrated a monolithic legacy backend to typed, containerized services without downtime.
highlights: Strangler-fig migration of 12 endpoints with zero downtime
Wrote the data-integrity test harness that caught 3 silent corruption bugs
Documented the old schema so well the archaeologists sent thanks

futureGoals: Ship an open-source Phaser chunk-streaming plugin
